human:
POINTS:
- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoExec.java:66] All that is advertised in the docs is that this config can be used to create a client.  I think it would be nice for the old config file to just pass it through and for the new config file only set the app name.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR splits fluo.properties into connection and application configs, adds multi-file loading support, and creates new command-line entry points. The documentation examples contain a critical completeness issue, and the multi-file loading capability appears to be missing from the public API.

POINTS:
- [docs/applications.md:30-35] **Documentation example is incomplete and will fail at runtime.** The second Java code example shows only `config.setInstanceZookeepers("localhost/fluo")` and `config.setApplicationName("myapp")`, but `hasRequiredClientProps()` (FluoConfiguration.java:547-553) requires four properties: application name, accumulo user, accumulo password, and accumulo instance. The `FluoClientImpl` constructor (core module) throws `IllegalArgumentException` when these are missing. The example must also call `setAccumuloUser()`, `setAccumuloPassword()`, and `setAccumuloInstance()` — or explain that `fluo-conn.properties` must contain these properties. The old code being removed (setAccumuloUser/setAccumuloPassword/setAccumuloInstance) was correct; replacing it with only setInstanceZookeepers/regApplicationName is a regression.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/SimpleConfiguration.java:63] **No public API for multi-file configuration loading.** The PR description claims "Support loading FluoConfiguration from multiple files" but SimpleConfiguration has no public method for adding configurations from multiple sources. The `load(InputStream in)` method (line 63) is private and adds to the internal CompositeConfiguration, but there is no public `addConfiguration()`, `load()`, or multi-file constructor. If the split config design requires loading fluo-conn.properties AND fluo-app.properties, this functionality must be added to the public API.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:115-132] **FluoConfiguration constructors delegate to SimpleConfiguration without multi-file support.** All constructors (empty, SimpleConfiguration, InputStream, File, Map) delegate to parent with a single source. No constructor accepts multiple files or streams. This limits the split-config pattern to "choose one file" rather than "layer connection + application configs".

- [docs/metrics.md] **Reference to fluo.properties not updated to reflect split config.** The metrics.md document (base content, line 24) still says "A Fluo application can be configured (in [fluo.properties]) to report metrics" and the link target references the single fluo.properties file. After the split, metrics config belongs in fluo-app.properties (application-level config), so this should be updated for consistency with the other doc changes.

- [modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/runnable/OracleRunnable.java:44 & WorkerRunnable.java:44] **Hardcoded path to `conf/fluo.properties` in YARN runnables.** Both OracleRunnable and WorkerRunnable reference `configDir + "/fluo.properties"` (line 44 in base). After the config split, these runnables need to load both fluo-conn.properties and fluo-app.properties, or the new combined configuration approach must be reflected here. This is a functional concern if the PR does not address it.

- [modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/util/FluoInstall.java:34-35] **FluoInstall.getFluoPropsPath() still returns `fluo.properties`.** The method returns `getFluoConfDir() + "/fluo.properties"`. If the PR splits config into two files, callers of `getFluoConfiguration()` (which loads this single file) will not get the full configuration. This utility class needs updating to reflect the new config structure.
v5:
SUMMARY:
This PR splits fluo.properties into connection/application properties, adds new standalone CLI commands (FluoInit, FluoOracle, FluoWorker, FluoScan, etc.), and deprecates the YARN/Twill-based cluster module. Changes span +3007/-866 lines across 67 files. The diff is truncated, preventing full verification of several new method definitions referenced by command classes.

POINTS:
- [modules/api/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfigurationTest.java:236] Test bug — `Assert.assertTrue(applicationProps.exists());` checks the wrong variable. The variable just declared on line 235 is `connectionProps`, so this should be `Assert.assertTrue(connectionProps.exists());`. This copy-paste error means the test passes even if fluo-conn.properties is missing, while silently ignoring the actual file being tested.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/FluoAdmin.java:42] Javadoc for `setClearZookeeper` still references deprecated `CLIENT_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT_PROP`. Should reference `CONNECTION_ZOOKEEPERS_PROP` (the new property added by this PR).

- [modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/runner/AppRunner.java:73] Breaking API change — `scan()` return type changed from `long` to `void`. The old method returned entry count; the new version delegates to `ScanUtil`. Any code depending on the return value will break at compile time.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoScan.java:125] Code duplication — `FluoScan.ScanOptions` duplicates JCommander field definitions that also exist in `AppRunner.ScanOptions` (newly added inner class). Three copies of identical scan option parsing exist across the codebase. Extract a single shared `ScanOptions` into fluo-core.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/SimpleConfiguration.java:166] Visibility change — `load(InputStream)` and `load(File)` changed from `private` to `public`. This expands the public API to support incremental multi-file loading, which is functional but was not mentioned in the PR description. Verify javadoc reflects this intentional API expansion.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/client/FluoAdminImpl.java:78] Constructor validation removed — `hasRequiredAdminProps()` check moved from constructor into `initialize()`. Callers building `FluoAdminImpl` with invalid config now get delayed failure at `initialize()` instead of immediate feedback. This behavioral shift may surprise existing users.

- [modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/command/FluoCommand.java:27] `@Deprecated` annotation lacks `since` attribute and has no Javadoc explaining what to use instead. Add `@deprecated` Javadoc tag pointing users to the new `modules/command` CLI classes (FluoInit, FluoOracle, etc.).

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:282-292] `getApplicationName()` now throws `NoSuchElementException` instead of the previous `IllegalArgumentException` from `getNonEmptyString()`. This exception type change could break callers that catch `IllegalArgumentException` for config validation. Consider keeping `IllegalArgumentException` for consistency.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:842-858] `hasRequiredMiniFluoProps()` checks both old (`CLIENT_ACCUMULO_*`) and new (`ACCUMULO_*`) prefixes for backward compatibility. Add a comment explaining why both prefix families are checked so future maintainers understand this is intentional migration logic.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoList.java:60] Uses `FluoAdminImpl.oracleExists(appConfig)` which is apparently converted from instance method to static method in this PR. Verify the static version doesn't require connection state that was previously available through the instance.

- NOTE: The diff is truncated after ~75K characters. New methods referenced throughout `modules/command` — specifically `FluoAdminImpl.isInitialized()`, `FluoAdminImpl.mergeZookeeperConfig()`, `FluoAdmin.getConnectionConfig()`, `FluoAdmin.getApplicationConfig()`, and the entire `ScanUtil` class — are defined in the truncated portion. The PR should not be merged until these definitions are confirmed present and correct.
